case_meta.source: fictional composite for FormSight content demonstration
case_meta.privacy_note: This is not a real person. It is a synthetic role built from common bullying-related life patterns for model testing.

profile.current_question: I want to know whether my difficulty showing myself, trusting relationships, and changing jobs is connected to what happened in school, and what my next three years may become.
self_description: I look normal and capable from the outside, but I feel very tense when I need to be seen by a group.
I can do detailed work, write good copy, and notice emotions quickly, but I avoid standing in the center.
I am afraid of being disliked suddenly, especially in relationships and group settings.
I often say it is fine, but then I disappear, withdraw, or over-explain.
I want a stable relationship and a visible career, but the moment I am evaluated, my body freezes first.

symbolic_anchors.label: 初二开始被小圈子孤立
symbolic_anchors.meaning: 被看见曾经意味着被嘲笑和攻击，后来逐渐形成低可见度生存策略。

symbolic_anchors.label: 不愿意在会议和社交平台主动出现
symbolic_anchors.meaning: 能力存在，但公开表达会触发旧的群体评价记忆。

symbolic_anchors.label: 伴侣冷淡时会迅速预判自己要被抛下
symbolic_anchors.meaning: 亲密关系触发旧的被排除感，关系预警系统过载。

symbolic_anchors.label: 从高中开始用日记记录观察
symbolic_anchors.meaning: 长期隐身也沉淀出细腻观察力和表达资产。

recent_events.text: The company asked her to lead a public campaign presentation, but she kept delaying the slide deck and finally gave the role to a colleague.
Her boyfriend replied coldly for two days. She did not ask directly, but checked old chat records, imagined being abandoned, and then became distant.
A friend did not invite her to a small dinner. Although there may have been no hostile intent, she felt the same bodily reaction as middle school exclusion.
A former colleague invited her to join a smaller but more creative content team where she would need to appear more often and own visible work.
When she needs to speak in group meetings, her stomach tightens, her shoulders freeze, and she tends to read the room before saying anything.

social_roles.description: She handles campaigns, writing, and coordination, but avoids being the face of the project.
Her family wants her to be stable, polite, and not make trouble.
She wants closeness but becomes highly sensitive to distance and silence.
In groups she reads micro-expressions quickly and tries to avoid becoming the target.

family_system.family_expectation: stable job, stable relationship, do not make conflicts public
family_system.unspoken_family_rule: If you are hurt outside, first check whether you did something wrong.

school_stages.state: quiet but academically good, usually praised for being obedient
social exclusion, rumor spreading, being laughed at when speaking in class
focused on grades and writing, kept a small distance from classmates
had a few close friends, avoided student organizations and public competition

education_path.teacher_evaluation: Teachers often saw her as quiet and sensible, but rarely noticed the relational harm behind the silence.
education_path.academic_identity: Grades became a safe way to prove value without becoming socially exposed.

intergenerational_family.family_repeated_pattern: The mother tends to endure and avoid conflict; the father values face and stability.
intergenerational_family.inherited_fear: Do not become the person who causes trouble or is disliked by others.
intergenerational_family.inherited_skill: Observe people's emotions early and keep the situation smooth.

place_mobility.belonging_split: The larger city gives opportunity, but also more comparison and more public evaluation.
place_mobility.mobility_desire: She wants a new identity outside the old classmate circle, but fears being seen again.

peer_authority.peer_relation_pattern: carefully friendly, avoids entering fixed small circles, rarely asks to be included
peer_authority.authority_relation_pattern: sensitive to leaders' tone; a small negative comment can freeze action for days
peer_authority.exclusion_memory: Middle school classmates created a private group without her, mocked her appearance and voice, and spread that she was pretending to be innocent.

class_money_shame.money_emotion: money means the right to leave situations without begging for acceptance
class_money_shame.class_memory: In middle school, classmates compared clothes and phones; she learned to hide what she lacked.
class_money_shame.shame_trigger: Being seen as cheap, awkward, or not fitting in.

body_image_gender.visibility_experience: She became very aware of how she looked, walked, spoke, and occupied space after being laughed at.
body_image_gender.gendered_pressure: She tries to be pleasant, clean, and not too aggressive, because being too visible once felt unsafe.
body_image_gender.body_signal: Freezing shoulders, stomach tightening, and shallow breathing in group evaluation settings.

time_rhythm.daily_rhythm: Good at late-night private work, weaker at daytime public action.
time_rhythm.stress_rhythm: When social threat rises, she loses sleep and rereads messages.
time_rhythm.recovery_need: Needs predictable solitude after social exposure.

digital_information.social_media_pattern: Watches other women's transformation and relationship content, saves many posts but rarely posts herself.
search_pattern: why am I afraid of being disliked
how to stop overthinking in relationships
digital_information.information_effect: Information helps her name the pattern but also keeps her analyzing instead of acting.

meaning_belief.meaning_source: She wants to turn sensitivity into a skill instead of a wound.
meaning_belief.belief_conflict: She believes people can change, but her body still expects groups to become dangerous.
meaning_belief.spiritual_or_symbolic_interest: Feels that some repeated relationship scenes are like life tests she keeps failing.

romantic_history.early_romance_template: First serious relationship in university felt safe because the other person was protective, but she became dependent on reassurance.
romantic_history.betrayal_or_abandonment_memory: A later partner went cold before breaking up, which fused silence with abandonment.
romantic_history.current_relationship_pattern: She wants direct reassurance but asks indirectly, then withdraws if the answer is not warm enough.
romantic_history.attraction_pattern: Attracted to calm, confident people, but also scared that confident people will judge her weakness.

worldview_orientation.world_assumption: Groups are not neutral; every group has hidden rankings and possible exclusion.
worldview_orientation.risk_model: Before acting, she predicts who may dislike her, who may laugh, and whether the cost of being visible is worth it.
worldview_orientation.reality_anchor: She knows not every group is dangerous, but her body still treats visibility as risk.

agency_control.agency_pattern: High private agency, low public agency.
agency_control.control_strategy: Prepare privately, avoid live exposure, make herself useful so she will not be attacked.
agency_control.best_intervention: small controlled visibility: one public artifact, one direct need expression, one repaired relationship conversation.

time_future_orientation.future_sense: Can imagine a better future, but only after social threat is lowered.
time_future_orientation.short_term_bias: Chooses immediate safety in groups even when long-term visibility would help her career.
time_future_orientation.future_block: Afraid that once she becomes visible, old exclusion will repeat in a more adult form.

observer_feedback: A close friend says she is very perceptive but disappears when she feels unwanted.
Her boyfriend says she seems calm, but suddenly becomes distant after small cold moments.
A colleague says her work is strong, but she gives away credit too easily.

health_and_energy.chronic_condition: stress-related stomach pain and shoulder tension during public evaluation

life_timeline.text: Praised for being obedient and sensible; learned that being low-conflict brings safety.
Middle school classmates formed a small circle, excluded her, mocked her voice and appearance, and laughed when she answered questions in class.
A rumor spread that she was pretending to be innocent. She stopped raising her hand and began checking classmates' expressions before speaking.
When she told her mother she was being isolated, her mother said maybe she should reflect on whether she was too sensitive. No clear repair happened.
She used grades and writing to rebuild value, but avoided public competitions and group leadership.
A university friend consistently included her and made her feel that not all groups are unsafe. This became a partial repair.
A partner became cold before breaking up. Silence began to feel like abandonment again.
At work she received praise for content planning, but avoided becoming the public project owner.
A new career opportunity requires visible ownership. She wants it, but the old fear of being judged has become the main threshold.

non_negotiables: not returning to a group where she is humiliated
having at least one relationship where needs can be spoken directly
value_system.value_conflict: She wants to be seen and recognized, but visibility still feels socially dangerous.

decision_style.under_pressure: read the room, withdraw, over-explain, then disappear
decision_style.risk_response: choose immediate social safety over long-term growth
decision_style.best_intervention: controlled visibility experiments and direct relationship clarification before emotional withdrawal

social_capital.current_circle: few close friends, weak group belonging, strong one-to-one sensitivity
trusted_observers: current boyfriend if communication is direct
social_capital.missing_network: safe professional circles that reward visible work without humiliation

intimacy_and_family_path.romantic_status: in a relationship but emotionally insecure
intimacy_and_family_path.private_life_need: a partner who can tolerate direct reassurance requests without shaming her sensitivity

wealth_path.asset_building_path: turn writing, brand work, emotional observation, and content planning into visible portfolio assets
wealth_path.transition_budget: needs a 90-day low-risk visibility ladder before leaving the stable role

macro_era.market_window: Her emotional observation and writing skill can become visible assets if she can tolerate controlled exposure.

names.label: last row seat
names.meaning: A safe place where she could observe without being targeted.

names.label: private notebook
names.meaning: Her unspoken self and the beginning of a possible public voice.

places.label: middle school corridor
places.meaning: A place where group gaze became dangerous.

places.label: Hangzhou office meeting room
places.meaning: The adult version of the same visibility test.

objects.label: muted group chat
objects.meaning: Wanting connection while protecting herself from exclusion signals.

digital_identities.label: private notes account
digital_identities.meaning: A possible bridge from hidden observation to public expression.

unfinished_artifacts.label: content portfolio draft
unfinished_artifacts.meaning: Visible identity has been prepared but not released.

long_texts: I know I am no longer in that classroom, but my body still reacts as if there is a small circle watching me. I want to be seen, but I also feel that being seen gives people a chance to attack me. In relationships, silence feels louder than words. At work, I can write and plan well, but when I need to stand in front of people, I feel I am thirteen again.

repeated_patterns: avoid public visibility even when ability is enough
give credit away to avoid becoming the target
use usefulness and politeness to stay safe
desired_future: a relationship where direct needs are safe
a career path where writing and observation become recognized assets
not living forever as the girl in the last row
feared_future: marrying someone who sees sensitivity as trouble
losing career opportunities because she cannot stand in front
repeating the same exclusion feeling in every adult group
